chapter_title.en: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them

text: 699 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ تَنَخَّمَ فِي اَلْمَسْجِدِ ثُمَّ رَدَّهَا فِي جَوْفِهِ لَمْ تَمُرَّ بِدَاءٍ إِلاَّ أَبْرَأَتْهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.699 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever clears his throat in the mosque and then swallows it back, no disease will pass through them without it curing them."
